question_text: A planet has a radius twice that of Earth and a mass 8 times greater. Calculate how the gravitational field strength (gravity) on this planet's surface compares to Earth's. (Take Earth's gravity as 10 N/kg)
explanation: Gravitational field strength g = GM/r². With mass 8 times greater and radius 2 times greater: g = 8M/(2r)² = 8M/4r² = 2 × (GM/r²). This means the planet's gravity is 2 × 10 = 20 N/kg, showing that both mass and radius significantly affect how strongly a planet pulls objects toward its surface.
